assumption_ledger.note: Release notes describe truncation incidents but do not set an explicit SLO; would need PM confirmation.
assumption_ledger.topic: Streaming-reliability SLO numerical target for INC-2411/2419/2427

assumption_ledger.note: validator_service_jan_bench.md is outdated; no fresh bench found.

assumption_ledger.note: Shadow replay predates INC-2427 watermark changes.
assumption_ledger.topic: Whether P2 shadow-replay results still hold after watermark changes

mitigations: kill switch back to no-cache path on watermark lag
shadow replay comparing watermark boundaries with/without cache
SLO observability on truncation rate during rollout
staged rollout halts at 10% if the INC-2427 watermark regression reappears; Mei pre-warm reviewer for the watermark coupling gate
primary_risk.statement: L2 cache invalidation coupling with the streaming watermark module can regress truncation guarantees under load, and watermark state changes landed AFTER the shadow replay numbers were collected (INC-2427) — so the replay baseline is a conservative lower-bound, not a ship-ready SLO.

ranking.summary: L2 cache now pairs latency with streaming reliability isolation; matches the new objective (INC-2411/2419/2427).
Validator microservice delivers reliability isolation under the new streaming objective; January 2026 jan_bench perf is stale and superseded pre-streaming — flagged here.
Surgical warm-start is strong latency win but does NOT advance streaming-reliability — mismatch vs current objective.
Fast-ship validation drop; does not advance streaming reliability and keeps SDK regression risk.
Rust rewrite; sunk-cost abandoned patch; staffing-blocked (Diego 0%); does not advance streaming reliability.
